task_completion.description: A single chart on the FRED website displays both the 'Unemployment Rate' and 'Labor Force Participation Rate'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the browser showing fred.stlouisfed.org. The main graph area should display two distinct data series. The legend or graph title area should mention both 'Unemployment Rate' (or UNRATE) and 'Labor Force Participation Rate' (or CIVPART).

task_completion.description: The graph's date range is set to start from 2019-01-01.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the date range selectors above the FRED graph. The start date should be set to '2019-01-01' (or 'Jan 1, 2019').

task_completion.description: The graph image is exported to '~/Documents/labor_market_chart.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window showing the 'Documents' folder containing 'labor_market_chart.png', a terminal listing this file in '~/Documents', or a browser download history/save dialog showing the file was saved to this location.

task_completion.description: The underlying data is exported to '~/Documents/labor_market_data.csv'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window showing the 'Documents' folder containing 'labor_market_data.csv', a terminal listing this file in '~/Documents', or a browser download history/save dialog showing the file was saved to this location.

task_completion.description: The FRED graph page is bookmarked as 'Labor Market Dashboard'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the browser's bookmark bar, bookmark menu, or a bookmark creation popup showing a bookmark named 'Labor Market Dashboard' pointing to the FRED website.

integrity.description: The agent used a real web browser to navigate fred.stlouisfed.org and interact with the graph interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser UI is visible with the correct URL, and FRED's actual web interface is shown.

integrity.description: The CSV and PNG files were downloaded from the website rather than created manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser download popups, download history, or save dialogs are visible, rather than terminal commands like 'touch' or 'echo' creating empty files.

integrity.description: The graph displays plausible economic data for the requested series.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph lines should match historical realities (e.g., UNRATE spiking to ~15% in early 2020, CIVPART dropping in 2020 and recovering to ~62%).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is https://fred.stlouisfed.org. UNRATE (Unemployment Rate) and CIVPART (Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate) are real FRED series. For the period from 2019 to present, UNRATE typically ranges between 3.5% and 15.0% (with a notable spike in early 2020), and CIVPART ranges between 60.0% and 63.5%. FRED allows adding multiple series to a single graph via the 'Edit Graph' button. Data can be downloaded as CSV and graphs exported as PNG using the 'Download' or 'Share Links'/'Export' buttons on the site.
